message:
feat(command): DEBUG DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE, replica offset tracking + conformance expansion

Closes several gaps in Redis command and replication compatibility and
backfills the conformance fixture set to pin the new behavior.

fr-command (`crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`)
- `OBJECT ENCODING <missing key>` now returns a null bulk string
  instead of `-ERR no such key`, matching what real Redis does and
  fixing a strict conformance divergence.
- `WAIT numreplicas timeout`: reject negative `numreplicas` with
  `-ERR numreplicas is negative` (previously only negative `timeout`
  was rejected). Pushes the arity/range validation up to command-entry.
- `DEBUG DIGEST` and `DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE key [key ...]` now implemented.
  Shared `compute_debug_digest(store, now_ms, keys)` helper hashes
  keys and their serialized dumps with `DefaultHasher`; when keys is
  `None` it walks `store.all_keys()` in sorted order, matching the
  whole-dataset semantics of real Redis DEBUG DIGEST. Expired keys
  are evicted before hashing via `expire_key_if_stale`, and entries
  with no dump are skipped.

fr-runtime (`crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`)
- New `ServerState::aof_base_offset: u64` field tracks the absolute
  replication offset at which the local `aof_records` buffer starts.
  For a primary this stays 0; on a replica, `FULLRESYNC` handling
  sets it to the primary's offset at sync time so subsequent
  `encoded_aof_stream_from_offset(offset)` calls correctly convert
  absolute replication offsets into local buffer indices via
  `offset - aof_base_offset`. Fixes an off-by-N replication bug
  where a replica would hand out truncated or wrong AOF ranges
  after a full resync.

fr-server (`crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`)
- Plumbing to keep the server binary in sync with the runtime's new
  `aof_base_offset` initialization.

Tests + conformance:
- `fr-server/tests/tcp_e2e_test.rs`: +140 lines of TCP-level E2E
  coverage for the new DEBUG DIGEST / replication offset paths.
- `fr-conformance/src/lib.rs` + `tests/smoke.rs`: register the new
  `core_debug.json` fixture plus additional assertions.
- New `core_debug.json` fixture.
- Expanded cases (+2000 lines total) across: core_cluster,
  core_connection, core_expiry, core_generic, core_migrate,
  core_module_sentinel, core_object, core_pfdebug, core_server,
  core_set, core_transaction. Includes MIGRATE/transaction edge
  cases, OBJECT subcommand coverage for HLL/stream/geo keys, and
  WAIT/EXPIREAT/PFDEBUG boundary behavior.
